note: HAND grips from either end — leading in HANDBAG, HANDCUFF and HANDSHAKE, trailing in FARMHAND and SECONDHAND. 'Female weapon' turning out to be a farm is the pivot I'd frame.

clues.parse: Hidden: caBBAGe conceals BAG — a sack. 'Found in' marks the concealment.
Double definition: to belt someone — a cuff round the ear — and the end of a shirt sleeve. 'Belt' reads as clothing but works as a verb.
Anagram: HAKES, off (gone bad, so rearranged), makes SHAKE — to tremble. Fish past its best would make anyone shudder.
Charade: F (female) + ARM (weapon) = FARM — quite literally a growing concern.
Double definition: a moment — just a second — and to second a motion, as in a meeting.
